# Read approach
<center>
![](/images/read.png){style="width:200px; background:white; border-radius:5px"}
</center>

There are two mains ways to analyse shotgun metagenomic datasets: read approaches, and assembly approaches.
This section will cover an introduction to the read approach.

## Taxonomic profiling
<center>
![](/images/classification.png){style="width:200px; background:white; border-radius:5px"}
</center>

We will first carry out taxonomic profiling with the following pages:

1. [Taxonomic profiling](/read_approach/taxonomic_profiling.qmd): Intro and start of taxonomic profiling part
2. [Kraken2](/read_approach/kraken2.qmd): Assign taxonomies to reads
3. [Krona](/read_approach/krona.qmd): Create interactive tiered pie charts read counts
4. [Bracken](/read_approach/bracken.qmd): Estimate abundances of taxa using the Kraken2 output
5. [LEfSe](/read_approach/lefse.qmd): Biomarker detection i.e. detected differential abundance of taxa based on sample groupings (Korean vs Western diet)

## Functional profiling
<center>
![](/images/profile.png){style="width:150px; background:white;border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
</center>

After taxonomic profiling we will carry out functional profiling.
In your future analysis you could run these in parallel.
The functional profiling workflow does not need output from the taxonomic profiling workflow and vice versa.

This part's pages are:

1. [Functional profiling](/read_approach/functional_profiling.qmd): Intro and overview of the HUMAnN pipeline
2. [HUMAnN](/read_approach/humann.qmd): Running of the HUMANnN pipeline for read based functional profiling
    - Includes description of output files
3. [Multi sample processing](/read_approach/multi_sample_processing.qmd): Combining, splitting, and normalising HUMANnN output
4. [Heatmap](/read_approach/heatmap.qmd): Creation of heatmap showing functional counts of the samples
5. [LEfSe](/read_approach/lefse_humann.qmd): LEfSe biomarker detection of the functional abundances
6. [Optional task](/read_approach/humann_task.qmd): Tasks related to different forms of normalisation
